Insert the spoken words after this in between the quotes. Wait for a pause to end sentence.

Spoken Phrase (Trigger): People use the word "quote" in natural sentences (e.g., "Can you give me a quote for the repairs?"). To prevent accidental intercepts, you can implement a brief pause-detection rule:
If the user says "quote" seamlessly in the middle of a rapid sentence, print the literal word: quote.
If the user pauses briefly (e.g., 300ms delay), says "quote", and continues, execute the intercept command: ".
Alternatively, forcing the user to say "open quote" instead of just "quote" drastically reduces accidental triggers and is the standard practice in professional dictation software.
